topic: humanoid robot supply chain: components, suppliers, actuators, sensors, batteries, semiconductors, end effectors, manufacturing, materials, trade policy

items.title: Renesas organizes its humanoid push around four subsystem domains
items.summary: The chipmaker is structuring its humanoid strategy into brain-and-motion, sensing, actuation and power management — betting suppliers will buy platforms rather than parts in a market it expects, according to Renesas, to double every year through 2030.

items.title: Agility's six-point policy plan puts the component gap first
items.summary: CEO Peggy Johnson urges Washington to close the gap on internationally sourced parts — citing McKinsey data that China holds roughly 90% of permanent-magnet motor capacity — in Agility's recommendations for a U.S. humanoid strategy.

items.title: Robot-hand maker Tesollo starts the IPO process after its Series B
items.summary: The Incheon-based end-effector specialist — whose five-fingered DG-5F-S weighs under 1 kg and costs about 60% less than its predecessor, according to the company — exports to 19 countries and is preparing to list next year.
